accounts.name: EIA
accounts.bio: Independent statistics and analysis from the U.S. Energy Information Administration. Official account. We try to respond to all the questions we receive here.

accounts.name: Jesse D. Jenkins
accounts.bio: Macro-energy systems engineering, optimization, and policy. Prof. @EPrinceton (MAE) & @AndlingerCenter. Co-host SHIFT KEY pod. See LinkedIn for more.

accounts.name: Grid Brief
accounts.bio: Energy. Power. Politics. A newsletter on fossil fuels, renewables, nuclear, and the grid. Read by investors, executives, engineers, policymakers, and more.

posts.content:
U.S. electricity net generation reached a record high of 4.43 TWh in 2025, up 2.8% from 2024 generation. #TodayInEnergy 

https://t.co/WsQvJzZkxz https://t.co/tIN0hQYeGv

U.S. developers signed sale and purchase contracts for a total of 5.2 Bcf/d of LNG in 2025, the highest volume since 7.0 Bcf/d in 2022. #TodayInEnergy #LNG

https://t.co/wqPJMqC3wK https://t.co/LoWOAIALy5

Japan's Kashiwazaki-Kariwa Unit 6 nuclear reactor restart last month will likely displace natural gas electricity generation, which accounted for 33% of Japan's electricity generation in 2024. #TodayInEnergy #nuclear

https://t.co/uDoizGzJjU https://t.co/16v64ER0Bj

New Electric Power Monthly with December 2025 data. Compared with December 2024:

⚡ U.S. net generation⬆️5.8%
⚡ Coal consumption for power⬆️5.0%
⚡ Avg residential retail price⬆️6.0%

https://t.co/UdG5MSb1va https://t.co/AtVEudpPsC

Most natural gas pipelines built in 2025 connect the South Central United States to supply. #TodayInEnergy #naturalgas

https://t.co/4MjQ3087vM https://t.co/Vhy7eTBaZw

The first LNG cargo from Sabine Pass was exported 10 years ago today. The United States is now the world’s largest LNG exporter, with U.S. LNG exports forecast to exceed 18.1 Bcf/d in 2027. #TodayInEnergy

https://t.co/a3WcdTWz8N https://t.co/n6p0MudQep

U.S. power plant owners and operators plan to retire nearly 11 GW of utility-scale electric generating capacity from the U.S. power grid this year. #TodayInEnergy #electricity

https://t.co/mNFJBCGPT7 https://t.co/43G2nmvJew

The federal government will be closed on February 16. The holiday release schedule next week will be:
 
🔵 Weekly Petroleum Status Report, Feb 19 at noon and 2:00 pm 
🔵 Heating Oil and Propane Update, Feb 19 at 2:00 pm
🔵 Winter Propane Market Update, Feb 19 after 2:00 pm
 
We will release all other publications as regularly scheduled.

Icon, the AI Admaker, just went bankrupt

They paid $12M for the domain https://t.co/MokmFd4GxD and now it's dead https://t.co/llpw7s1lgW

Dodgy stuff surrounding this

No social media posts, no press releases and no communication

Yet they keep rebilling customers just days ago!

Clothing stores are so useless these days

You visit the brand store to get a specific thing you saw and you wanna buy it

You arrive, and it's either out of stock, or it's not in your size

Like they're not even trying to compete with online stores that have every single thing in every single size in stock forever

Why???
I KNEW IT

3 fake abuse reports in 24 hours on my Hetzner server

All SYN IP spoofed packages port scanning on port 22, which isn't me because it's just spoofing my IP

Every day I have to reply to @Hetzner_Online now that it's not me, I can't port scan 22 outbound, because I have 22 outbound blocked on their firewall, if I don't reply within 24h my IP gets blocked!

I guess I need to speak to someone at Hetzner internally to fix this because the internet has lots of ppl that get this issue

If Hetzner can fix this, they'll be a great host for the future I think
Yubikeys and Trezors spotted

💸 @X paid me a reasonable $12,000 to tweet this month

$10,511 ad rev share
$1,247 subs revenue
(= $11,758 per 28 days)

= $12,807/month

Median X revenue now since X started paying me is @ $9,000/mo

If my X account was a business and valued at just these payouts, it would be worth at 10x @ $1,080,000, or at 20x @ $2,160,000

Although I think a bit more if you value the reach

Any way I can avoid my IP getting spoofed?

Can hosting providers check if abuse reports are based on real or spoofed packages?
Great post on what happened to me https://t.co/wGV68ZfAQL

A Brazilian today told me she visited Europe a week ago

She says she felt more unsafe in Europe than the city were she's from in South Brazil, Curitiba, which is pretty safe

She felt most unsafe in Belgium and the Netherlands

Which is really sad to hear as a Dutch person that it has come to this

She was followed by three guys after getting off at the train, who were signalling things to each other as if they were gonna try rob her or worse

She then fled to a group of local guys to act like she was their friend so they got away from her

Of course one thing to note she was in the center of Amsterdam and Brussels, which you wouldn't wish to anybody, but then again it's crazy we can't keep our centers in Europe safe anymore

In North and South America, downtown cities are generally like this, full of crime, junkies, etc, Brazil of course too, but Europe's centers used to be nice and historical and cute and clean and SAFE!
Of course I did not pass the opportunity to spam her my site https://t.co/Mb2FUp6cuW
